category_title: Students with high Callous-Unemotional (CU) traits, proactive aggression, and reduced emotional responsiveness
minimal_context: The student is participating in a 15-minute LGS curriculum lesson in the classroom to develop working memory and attention.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's begin our daily LGS curriculum work to practice our working memory and attention.

STUDENT: I'm not doing the LGS curriculum work today.

TUTOR: Please look at the 'Ready to Learn' thermometer on your desk and tell me where you are.

STUDENT: I don't care about the thermometer! I don't understand what my emotions mean for my behavior!

TUTOR: You need to sit down and focus on the task.

STUDENT: (Throws the materials and kicks the chair, escalating into an overloaded behavior) I can't find the stop button!

options.A: Encourage compassionate attitudes towards peers by gaining an understanding of their needs, emotions, capacities, and similarities with themselves.
options.B: Establish a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for evidence-based exchange of viewpoints.
options.C: Subdivide the LGS curriculum work into 15-minute segments and introduce short breaks between learning activities.
options.D: Require the student to take an LGS progress report home to parents and return it signed to the tutor.
